problem_statement.title: Fix resolution bug with empty panels
problem_statement.body:
This PR aims to fix #6758.

In #6065, I erroneously used `drop = FALSE`, whereas I should've stuck to the #5632 solution to this problem.
problem_statement.issue_title: `geom_col` does not render in facets in `ggplot2` 4.0.1
problem_statement.issue_body:
### Description:
In `ggplot2` version 4.0.1, when using `geom_col()` in a `facet_grid()`, the plot panel for the second facet renders blank. This issue does not occur in `ggplot2` version 4.0.0, where the same plot renders correctly. Replacing `geom_col()` with other geoms, like `geom_point()`, works as expected.

### Steps to reproduce:

``` r
# Load necessary libraries
library(remotes)
library(fs)
library(tidyverse)
library(glue)

# Check the version of ggplot2 package in use
packageVersion('ggplot2')  
#> [1] '4.0.1'

# Prepare the data
df <- 
  airquality %>% 
  summarise(.by = c(Month, Day),
            Ozone = mean(Ozone),
            Temp = mean(Temp),
  ) %>% 
  mutate(date = glue("{Month}-{Day}-1973"),
         date = parse_date_time(date, 
                                "%m-%d-Y",
                                tz = "UTC")) %>% 
  select(-Month, -Day) %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = -date) 

# Test plot with `ggplot2` version 4.0.1 where `geom_col` fails to render
ggplot(data = df,
       aes(
         x = date,
         y = value
       )) +
  facet_grid(name ~ ., scales = "free") +
  geom_line(data = \(x) filter(x, name == "Ozone")) +
  geom_col(data = \(x) filter(x, name == "Temp")) 
#> Warning in min(x): no non-missing arguments to min; returning Inf
#> Warning in max(x): no non-missing arguments to max; returning -Inf
```

![](https://i.imgur.com/nhaAUV4.png)<!-- -->

``` r


# Test plot with `geom_point` instead of `geom_col` (this works fine)
ggplot(data = df,
       aes(
         x = date,
         y = value
       )) +
  facet_grid(name ~ ., scales = "free") +
  geom_line(data = \(x) filter(x, name == "Ozone")) +
  geom_point(data = \(x) filter(x, name == "Temp")) 
```

![](https://i.imgur.com/ZocbbDo.png)<!-- -->

``` r


# Now, let's detatch the current `ggplot2` version and install version 4.0.0
detach("package:ggplot2")

# Create a new folder to install an older version of the package.
old_lib <- "~/old_lib/"

# Install the older version of ggplot2 (4.0.0)
install_version("ggplot2", version = "4.0.0", lib = old_lib)

# Load the older version of `ggplot2`
library("ggplot2", lib.loc = old_lib)
#> Overwriting method +(<ggplot2::ggplot>, <ANY>)
#> Overwriting method +(<ggplot2::theme>, <ANY>)
#> Overwriting method convert(<ggplot2::ggplot>, <list>)

# Check the version of `ggplot2` now
packageVersion("ggplot2")
#> [1] '4.0.0'

# Test plot again using ggplot2 version 4.0.0 (this should work as expected)
ggplot(data = df,
       aes(
         x = date,
         y = value
       )) +
  facet_grid(name ~ ., scales = "free") +
  geom_line(data = \(x) filter(x, name == "Ozone")) +
  geom_col(data = \(x) filter(x, name == "Temp")) 
#> Warning in min(x): no non-missing arguments to min; returning Inf
#> Warning in min(x): no non-missing arguments to max; returning -Inf
#> Warning in min(d[d > tolerance]): no non-missing arguments to min; returning
#> Inf
```

![](https://i.imgur.com/Aylfgph.png)<!-- -->

``` r


reprex::reprex(session_info = TRUE)
#> ℹ Non-interactive session, setting `html_preview = FALSE`.
#> CLIPR_ALLOW has not been set, so clipr will not run interactively
#> Error in switch(where, expr = stringify_expression(x_expr), clipboard = ingest_clipboard(), : EXPR must be a length 1 vector
```

<sup>Created on 2025-11-25 with [reprex v2.1.1](https://reprex.tidyverse.org)</sup>

patch:
diff --git a/DESCRIPTION b/DESCRIPTION
index 01f91fcb9a..da78c46c3d 100644
--- a/DESCRIPTION
+++ b/DESCRIPTION
@@ -70,7 +70,7 @@ Suggests:
     rpart,
     sf (>= 0.7-3),
     svglite (>= 2.1.2),
-    testthat (>= 3.1.5),
+    testthat (>= 3.3.0),
     tibble,
     vdiffr (>= 1.0.6),
     xml2
diff --git a/NEWS.md b/NEWS.md
index a252538302..714784d06f 100644
--- a/NEWS.md
+++ b/NEWS.md
@@ -4,6 +4,8 @@
   (@lgaborini, #6724)
 * Added new argument `geom_curve(shape)` that will be passed down to 
   `grid::curveGrob()` (@fmarotta, #5998).
+* Fixed a regression where default `width` was miscalculated when some panels
+  are empty (@teunbrand, #6758)
 
 # ggplot2 4.0.1
 
diff --git a/R/utilities.R b/R/utilities.R
index 598b9a8fc3..2aa6ab5b42 100644
--- a/R/utilities.R
+++ b/R/utilities.R
@@ -927,7 +927,7 @@ compute_data_size <- function(data, size, default = 0.9,
   panels <- arg_match0(panels, c("across", "by", "ignore"))
 
   if (panels == "across") {
-    res <- split(data[[var]], data$PANEL, drop = FALSE)
+    res <- split(data[[var]], data$PANEL, drop = TRUE)
     res <- vapply(res, resolution, FUN.VALUE = numeric(1), ...)
     res <- min(res, na.rm = TRUE)
   } else if (panels == "by") {
test_patch:
diff --git a/tests/testthat/test-utilities.R b/tests/testthat/test-utilities.R
index 66e380c973..33fb9fb5cc 100644
--- a/tests/testthat/test-utilities.R
+++ b/tests/testthat/test-utilities.R
@@ -197,3 +197,13 @@ test_that("fallback_palette finds palettes", {
   pal <- fallback_palette(sc)
   expect_true(is_discrete_pal(pal))
 })
+
+test_that("compute_data_size handles gnarly cases", {
+  # Test missing levels
+  df <- data_frame0(
+    x = seq(0, 20, by = 2),
+    PANEL = factor(rep("B", 11), levels = c("A", "B"))
+  )
+  new <- compute_data_size(df, size = NULL, target = "width", default = 1)
+  expect_all_equal(new$width, 2)
+})

fail_to_pass: test_that("compute_data_size handles gnarly cases", { df <- data_frame0(x = seq(0, 20, by = 2), PANEL = factor(rep("B", 11), levels = c("A", "B"))); new <- compute_data_size(df, size = NULL, target = "width", default = 1); expect_all_equal(new$width, 2) })
pass_to_pass: Existing tests for compute_data_size with normal panel data

key_changes: Change `drop = FALSE` to `drop = TRUE` in the `split()` call within `compute_data_size()` function
This ensures empty factor levels are dropped before calculating resolution
potential_pitfalls: Using `drop = FALSE` preserves empty factor levels, which causes `resolution()` to return Inf or incorrect values for empty vectors
The fix must maintain backward compatibility with panels that have all levels populated

instruction: Fix the bug in `compute_data_size()` function in R/utilities.R where the default `width` is miscalculated when some panels are empty. The issue occurs when using `geom_col()` in a `facet_grid()` where some panels have no data - the plot renders blank instead of showing the data. The bug was introduced by incorrectly using `drop = FALSE` in the `split()` call when calculating resolution across panels. The fix should ensure that empty factor levels are properly handled.
context:
The `compute_data_size()` function calculates data dimensions (width/height) for geoms. When `panels = "across"`, it splits data by PANEL factor and calculates resolution. The relevant code section:

r
if (panels == "across") {
    res <- split(data[[var]], data$PANEL, drop = FALSE)  # BUG: should be drop = TRUE
    res <- vapply(res, resolution, FUN.VALUE = numeric(1), ...)
    res <- min(res, na.rm = TRUE)
}


When a PANEL factor has levels with no corresponding data (e.g., levels c("A", "B") but only "B" has data), using `drop = FALSE` creates an empty vector for the missing level, which causes `resolution()` to return unexpected values.
